seo.title: AP Psych Curve 2026 | AP Psychology Score Conversion Explained
seo.description: Learn how the AP Psych curve works and how raw MCQ and FRQ scores may convert to an estimated AP Psychology score from 1 to 5.

content.directAnswer: The AP Psych curve is best understood as an estimated raw-to-scaled score conversion. Actual cutoffs can vary, so use the calculator for planning rather than official scoring.

sections.heading: What Is the AP Psych Curve?
sections.body: Students often call the score conversion a curve, but AP scores are converted from exam performance into a 1 to 5 score using processes that can vary by year.

sections.heading: Raw Score to AP Score Conversion
sections.body: Your MCQ and FRQ results combine into a composite estimate. This site uses estimated score bands to help you understand possible outcomes.

faqs.question: Is this calculator official?
faqs.answer: No. This calculator is an independent estimate and is not affiliated with or endorsed by College Board.

faqs.question: Can actual AP score cutoffs vary?
faqs.answer: Yes. Actual score thresholds can vary by year and exam form, so this tool should be used for planning only.

faqs.question: Does FRQ performance matter for AP Psych?
faqs.answer: Yes. FRQ performance contributes to the composite estimate, so a stronger FRQ score can offset some missed multiple-choice questions.

faqs.question: Should I use this before or after the exam?
faqs.answer: You can use it before the exam for target planning or after the exam to estimate possible score outcomes.
